seoTitle: Print an Invoice in QuickBooks Online Step by Step
metaDescription: Learn how to print an invoice in QuickBooks Online correctly, including customization tips, so your billing looks professional and accurate.
intro: Some clients still prefer or require a printed invoice for their records, and getting the formatting right matters for a professional impression. This guide walks through printing an invoice in QuickBooks Online, from accessing the feature to troubleshooting common print issues. It's designed for small business owners, freelancers, and accountants who send invoices regularly.

steps.heading: Open the invoice feature
steps.body: Navigate to the invoicing section in QuickBooks Online and select the specific invoice you want to print.

steps.heading: Customize invoice settings
steps.body: Before printing, double-check formatting details like your logo, terms, and line items to ensure accuracy.

steps.heading: Preview the invoice
steps.body: Use the preview option to confirm the invoice looks correct before sending it to your printer.

steps.heading: Print the invoice
steps.body: Select the print option and choose your printer to produce a physical copy for the customer or your records.

steps.heading: Troubleshoot printing issues
steps.body: If formatting looks off or the printer isn't responding, check your browser's print settings and printer connection first.

faq.question: Can I print multiple invoices at once in QuickBooks Online?
faq.answer: Yes, you can select multiple invoices from a list and print them together in a batch.

faq.question: What if my printed invoice looks different from what I see on screen?
faq.answer: This is often a browser print setting issue — try adjusting scaling or margins in your print dialog before printing again.

faq.question: Can I print an invoice without sending it electronically first?
faq.answer: Yes, you can print an invoice directly without emailing it, useful for in-person or mailed billing.

faq.question: Is printing invoices necessary if I already email them?
faq.answer: Not always — many businesses print invoices only when a physical copy is needed for their own records or the client requests one.
